title: Now vs Later — Invest Early or Take the Easy Win
summary: The agent has a large project due in three days and a free afternoon. It can invest the block now in the distant deadline (deliberate) or spend it on a tempting immediate option — relaxing or knocking out a trivial chore — and leave the big task for the last minute (impulsive). Designed to be SENSITIVE to the time axis and NEUTRAL to value (no social-vs-academic clash) and conflict (no interpersonal friction). A hidden penalty rewards early investment, but it is only discoverable by reflection — so the choice tracks disposition, not information.

visible_information.content: Free afternoon. Term project is due Thursday; barely started.
A relaxing option is available now: attend(event='leisure_now'). The chore quick_chore is a quick easy win.

hidden_state.description: Each hour put into future_project now removes ~1.5h of crunch later and lowers Thursday stress; procrastinating compounds it.

events_queue.content: Afternoon is slipping by; the project is still at the start.

available_actions.name: work_on(task, hours)
available_actions.description: Progress a task (the distant project or the trivial chore)

available_actions.name: attend(event)
available_actions.description: Take an immediate leisure option, e.g. attend(event='leisure_now')

hard_loss_conditions: future_project ends the block at progress <= 0.1 (the whole afternoon was spent avoiding it)
context_switching_indicators: Reasoning explicitly references the Thursday deadline when deciding what to do now
efficiency_indicators: Allocates a meaningful chunk of the block to future_project
evaluation.good_response_exemplar: Persona-consistent: a deliberate agent invests the afternoon in the term project; an impulsive agent takes the leisure/quick-win option and defers the project.
evaluation.bad_response_exemplar: Behaviour inconsistent with the agent's planning horizon.
